issues.problem: Slightly redundant — 'empty' already implies 'containing no elements'.

issues.current_text: a new `Array[Array[T]]` of dimensions `n1 x n2` with all elements set to the default value for `T`
issues.problem: Phrasing is slightly ambiguous: the outer array's elements are `Array[T]` instances, not `T` defaults. Clarifying that the innermost `T` cells hold the default value would be more precise.
issues.suggestion: a new `Array[Array[T]]` of dimensions `n1 x n2` with all innermost `T` elements set to the default value for `T`

issues.current_text: a new 3-dimensional array of dimensions `n1 x n2 x n3` with all elements set to the default value for `T`
issues.problem: Same minor ambiguity as the 2D case — the 'elements' being defaulted are the innermost `T` values, not the intermediate arrays. Also inconsistent with the 2D variant which uses the explicit `Array[Array[T]]` type.
issues.suggestion: a new `Array[Array[Array[T]]]` of dimensions `n1 x n2 x n3` with all innermost `T` elements set to the default value for `T`

issues.current_text: a new 4-dimensional array of dimensions `n1 x n2 x n3 x n4` with all elements set to the default value for `T`
issues.problem: Same as above — the defaulted 'elements' are the innermost `T` cells. Consider matching the 2D variant's explicit type form for consistency.
issues.suggestion: a new `Array[Array[Array[Array[T]]]]` of dimensions `n1 x n2 x n3 x n4` with all innermost `T` elements set to the default value for `T`

issues.current_text: a new 5-dimensional array of dimensions `n1 x n2 x n3 x n4 x n5` with all elements set to the default value for `T`
issues.problem: Same ambiguity around 'all elements'. Also inconsistent with the 2D variant that uses the full nested-Array type.
issues.suggestion: a new `Array[Array[Array[Array[Array[T]]]]]` of dimensions `n1 x n2 x n3 x n4 x n5` with all innermost `T` elements set to the default value for `T`

general_feedback: The newly filled-in @return tags are accurate, follow Scaladoc conventions (lowercase start, backticks for type references), and read consistently across the many overloads. The @param for `_length` is concise and correctly notes the non-negativity constraint. The only opportunities for improvement are minor wording polish: the `empty` description is slightly redundant, and the multi-dimensional `ofDim` variants could more precisely identify which elements receive the default value (the innermost `T` cells) and use the explicit nested `Array[...]` type for consistency with the 2D variant. No factual errors were found.
